title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: Amongst them was Safwan bin Umayyah who ямВed Makkah. He was confused as to where he should head to, so he decided to go to Jeddah to board a ship and sail to Yemen. When the people noticed the generous pardoning on part of the Messenger of Allah яБе, and the fact that he decided to overlook the painful past, there came to him тАШUmayr bin Wahb, who Forgiving others said, тАЬO Prophet of Allah, Safwan bin Umayyah is the leader of his people. He has ямВed Makkah in fear of you and thrown himself into the sea. Please, grant him security! May AllahтАЩs blessings be on you!тАЭ The Prophet яБе quite comfortably said, тАЬHe is granted security.тАЭ тАШUmayr said, тАЬO Messenger of Allah, please give me a sign with which he will know that you have granted him security.тАЭ The Prophet яБе gave him his turban which he wore upon entering Makkah, so that when Safwan saw it, he would trust тАШUmayrтАЩs words. тАШUmayr took the turban and caught up with Safwan as he was trying to board the ship. He said, тАЬO Safwan! May my father and mother be sacriямБced for you! For AllahтАЩs sake, donтАЩt kill yourself! I have come to you with an assurance of security from the Messenger of Allah!тАЭ Safwan said, тАЬWoe be to you! Disappear from my sight and donтАЩt speak to me! You are a liar!тАЭ He was afraid of the consequences of the evil actions he had perpetrated against the Muslims. тАШUmayr shouted, тАЬO Safwan! May my father and mother be sacriямБced for you! The Messenger of Allah is the best of people! He is the most righteous of people! He is the most persevering of people! He is the most excellent of people! He is your cousin. His dignity is your dignity. His honour is your honour. His kingdom is your kingdom!тАЭ Safwan said, тАЬI fear for my life.тАЭ Forgiving others тАШUmayr replied, тАЬHe is too persevering and honouring to exact revenge!тАЭ Safwan therefore returned with him and they reached Makkah. тАШUmayr took him to the Messenger of Allah яБе. Safwan said, тАЬHe claims that you have granted me security.тАЭ The Prophet яБе replied, тАЬHe has spoken the truth.тАЭ Safwan said, тАЬAs for accepting Islam, then please give me two months to think about it,тАЭ i.e. I will remain in Makkah on my religion of worshipping idols for two months and contemplate whether or not I should accept Islam. The Prophet яБе said, тАЬYou can think for four months.тАЭ Safwan later embraced Islam тАУ may Allah be pleased with him. How beautiful it is to forgive others and forget the painful past! Without any doubt, only great people can have this characteristic - those who rise with their noble principles above the lowliness of revenge and retribution, grudge and rage. Life is very short. It is surely too short for us to soil it with grudge and malice. Even with regards to personal matters, the Prophet яБе was very easy going. Al-Miqdad bin al-Aswad said, тАЬMy friends and I arrived in Madinah and met people, but nobody accepted us as guests. We thus came to the Prophet яБе and mentioned to him what had happened. He hosted us in his house where he had four goats and said: тАШMilk them, O Miqdad. Divide them up in four portions and have one each.тАЩ This is what I would do each day.тАЭ Forgiving others Every evening, al-Miqdad would milk the goats, drink from them, along with his friends, and leave a portion for the Prophet яБе. If the Prophet яБе was present he would drink it there and then, and if he was absent, they would preserve it for him until he яБе returned. One evening, al-Miqdad milked the goats as usual and portioned it into four, drunk it along with his friends and left a portion for the Prophet яБе to drink when he returned. The Prophet яБе, however, got delayed. Al-Miqdad laid down in his bed and thought to himself, тАЬPerhaps, the Prophet яБе went to some AnsariтАЩs house where they gave him dinner. Maybe I can drink his portion of milk!тАЭ His soul kept whispering to him until he eventually got up and drunk it, not leaving anything for the Prophet яБе! Al-Miqdad said, тАЬWhen the milk entered my stomach, I was very much taken by what had happened and felt remorse. I thought to myself that perhaps the Prophet яБе would arrive at any time, hungry and thirsty, only to ямБnd his utensil empty, and thereupon he may even pray against me! I covered my face with my blanket.тАЭ, (due to sorrow). After a while, the Prophet яБе came and greeted people loud enough for those who were awake to hear without disturbing those who were asleep. Al-Miqdad was still lying in his bed looking at the Prophet яБе. The Prophet яБе turned to his utensil to ямБnd it empty.
